title: taraxacum clemens
intro: Taraxacum clemens, commonly known as the common dandelion, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to Europe and Asia but now found worldwide. The plant's medicinal properties lie in its roots, leaves, and flowers, which contain vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals like potassium and iron. The health benefits of taraxacum clemens include its ability to support liver and kidney function, improve digestion, and act as a natural diuretic, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of taraxacum clemens can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
